hook: The dorm move-in mistake that costs you 45 minutes

scenes.visual_prompt: Time-lapse of a family unpacking a closet and desk first, boxes piling up on the floor behind them
scenes.voiceover: Here's the sequencing mistake I see almost every family make on move-in day.

scenes.visual_prompt: Frustrated family standing in a room full of open boxes with nowhere left to put the overflow
scenes.on_screen_text: 45 min in: nowhere to put anything
scenes.voiceover: They unpack the closet and desk first, then realize there's no room for the overflow.

scenes.visual_prompt: Two rigid under-bed bins being slid under a twin XL frame before anything else is unpacked
scenes.voiceover: Under-bed storage goes in before a single box gets opened, or you do the room twice.

scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of a soft-sided fabric bin with a mesh top window, hand unzipping to reveal shoes inside
scenes.voiceover: I added a set of fabric bins with mesh windows on top, so you can see what's inside without unzipping.

scenes.visual_prompt: Four bins total lined up, then slid under the bed together
scenes.voiceover: Between four bins total, we stored what would've been three full boxes of overflow.

scenes.visual_prompt: Tape measure showing the gap between the floor and the underside of a dorm bed frame
scenes.voiceover: If your school's frame sits lower than 6 inches, measure before you buy rigid bins.

scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot of a completely clear dorm room floor, one-hour mark clock overlay
scenes.voiceover: Do storage first and the floor stays clear the whole move-in.

caption: Do this before you unpack a single box. The bins I used (and the one measurement that saves you a return) are on the blog.
